title: Transmission Electron Microscopy (TEM) Protocol for Pseudomonas sp.
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2dlju","text":"This protocol describes the preparation, fixation, staining, and imaging of Pseudomonas sp. using Transmission Electron Microscopy (TEM). The method provides high-resolution visualization of bacterial morphology and flagellar structures, confirming motility-related features. Proper fixation and staining ensure cellular integrity and contrast for high-quality micrographs","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":76,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"937s4","text":"Ensure all sample preparation steps are performed under sterile conditions to avoid contamination.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"30gcv","text":"Use appropriate PPE (gloves, lab coat, and safety glasses) when handling glutaraldehyde and UranyLess stain.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f1sk1","text":"Avoid excessive drying of the bacterial suspension before staining to preserve cellular integrity.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1tcrj","text":"Handle TEM grids carefully using fine-point forceps to prevent structural damage.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"amcf8","text":"Ensure the microscope is properly calibrated before imaging to obtain high-resolution micrographs.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"9isfd","text":"Prepare all reagents and solutions in advance.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4rrmn","text":"Label sample tubes and TEM grids properly.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"65ne1","text":"Ensure the TEM microscope is ready for imaging with proper alignment.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a4c7b","text":"Work in a clean and contamination-free workspace.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"3bkii","text":"Glutaraldehyde is toxic and should be handled under a fume hood with proper PPE.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"codh7","text":"UranyLess stain is a heavy metal-based solution; proper disposal procedures must be followed.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"13eb4","text":"Avoid direct contact with TEM grids, as fingerprints and static can interfere with imaging.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fq7ud","text":"Ensure all biohazardous waste (bacterial cultures and used grids) is disposed of according to institutional guidelines.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Bacterial Culture Preparation Grow Pseudomonas sp. in LB Lite Broth under optimal conditions for 24 hours. Harvest bacterial cells by centrifugation at an appropriate speed (e.g., 10,000 x g for 5 minutes). Carefully discard the LB medium, retaining only the bacterial pellet. Note: Fresh cultures yield the best results for TEM imaging.

Washing and Fixation Wash the bacterial pellet twice with DDI water to remove residual media. Fix bacterial cells overnight in 4% glutaraldehyde prepared in 0.1M Sodium Cacodylate Buffer (pH 7.4) at 4°C. Note: Fixation preserves the bacterial morphology for high-resolution imaging.

Sample Preparation for TEM Pipette 20 µL of bacterial suspension onto a formvar-coated copper grid. Allow the bacterial sample to adhere for 30 seconds. Carefully remove excess liquid using filter paper or fine-point forceps. Apply 20 µL of UranyLess stain to the grid for 60 seconds to enhance contrast. Remove excess stain and allow the grid to air dry before imaging. Note: Ensure gentle handling to avoid cell damage during staining and drying.

Imaging and Analysis Examine the prepared bacterial sample using a 120 kV FEI T12 Tecnai Spirit TEM. Capture high-resolution micrographs to visualize: Overall cell morphology Flagellar structure (monotrichous flagellum) 3. Analyze images to confirm motility-related structures and bacterial surface features. Note: Adjust TEM settings for optimal contrast and resolution.

Expected Results High-resolution TEM micrographs showing detailed bacterial morphology and flagellar structure. Well-preserved cellular integrity due to proper fixation and staining. No significant sample aggregation or damage, ensuring accurate visualization.
creator: Md Sahadat Ali
